The extended final scene is a spontaneous conversation among Martin Scorsese, Harvey Keitel, Jay Cocks and Verna Bloom who, along with a large group of NYU students, found themselves frustrated and perplexed by the events and hopeful that the protests would result in change.
Genres: Documentary, Historical Drama, Politics, Social Commentary
Themes: 1970s counterculture, USA, protest, social change, student activism, war

Title: The Sweet Creek County War
Description: In this western, a sheriff gets tired of upholding the law and retires to a quiet ranch. Unfortunately, he ends up saving his homesteader neighbors from a corrupt Wyoming land grabber.
Themes: USA, corruption, frontier life, law and order

Title: Bonjour Balwyn
Description: Kevin Agar has left a job in insurance to start his own magazine, called Bolo. His middle class parents dont understand it, and very few people buy it. As his debts mount, Kevin loses his girlfriend, his friends and his secretary.
Themes: AUSTRALIA, career struggles, family disapproval, financial hardship, isolation, relationship loss

Title: Disco Fever / AKA Jukebox
Description: Ex-teen idol Desmond and his comical manager get mixed up with a jaded night club owner who wants to sign Desmond as the opening act on a swingin’ Acapulco bound disco plane. No “Top 40” hits to be found, this film contains completely original songs and music. Featuring a cameo by Adolfo “Shabba-Doo” Quinones ofBreakin’fame!
Themes: Acapulco, USA, celebrity cameos, disco plane, night club owner, original music, teen idol

Title: Demolition
Description: When British Intelligence, following a series of experiments designed to increase alertness of long-distance lorry drivers through use of tonal repetition, discovers chordation that completely erases all memory, it is obvious that such a cleansed mind may be programmed to perform any type of action. Erstwhile agent Peter Clarke inactive for years, is chosen as courier to transport an audio tape of the tones to his native Australia.
Genres: TV Movie, psychological drama, sci-fi thriller
Themes: AUSTRALIA, identity loss, memory manipulation, mind control, travel and displacement

Title: Heat
Description: “Heat” is a parody of “Sunset Boulevard.” Joey Davis, an unemployed ex-child actor, uses sex to get his landlady, Lydia, to reduce his rent, and then tries to exert his influence on Sally Todd, who is now washed-up and wasn’t even more than slightly important at the height of her career. Sally tries to help Joey, until he realizes that she just isn’t well-connected enough to be of any service to him. The affair is complicated by Sally’s psychotic, maybe-lesbian-or-maybe-not daughter Jessica, who tries to muscle in on her mother’s relationship with Joey.
Genres: Cult, Dark Comedy, Drama, Parody, Satire
Themes: Hollywood decline, USA, exploitation of youth, failed ambition, lesbian identity, mother-daughter dynamics, psychological instability, sexual manipulation

Title: Who Killed Mary Whats’ername? / AKA Death of a Hooker
Description: A prostitute is murdered on the streets of a tough, low-income neighborhood. A diabetic retired boxer who knew her is appalled by the lack of interest shown in the case by the police or anybody else in the neighborhood, and decides to investigate the case himself.
Themes: USA, class struggle, crime and punishment, personal justice, redemption, social injustice

Title: Hoshizora no marionette / AKA Puppets Under Starry Skies
Description: Hideo is the leader of the motorcycle gang The Bears. He beats up the leader of a rival gang The Scorpions without a reason. The Scorpions retaliate, which lands Hideo severly injured in the hospital of Hiroshi’s father. Hiroshi, the black sheep of his wealthy family is a gay boy with a drug addiction, who’s deeply in love with the cool, self-assured Hideo. But Hideo ignores Hiroshi’s affection and dates Akemi, the promiscuous daughter of a hooker mother. Thus an unhappy love triangle develops.
Themes: JAPAN, addiction, family dynamics, gang violence, identity, love triangle, rejection, social class

Title: Quatre nuits d’un rêveur / AKA Four Nights of a Dreamer
Description: Jacques is an artist whose world changes when he meets Marthe, a woman intent on taking her own life. After he stops her from doing a swan dive into the Seine River, the two start regularly meeting, and Jacques learns that Marthe almost killed herself over a broken heart. As Jacques and Marthe wander through Paris over a series of nights, he begins to fall in love with her — only to find that her former lover is not entirely out of the picture.
Themes: FHD, FRANCE, HD, Parisian life, emotional turmoil, love and loss, redemption

Title: Hail
Description: A presidential advisor discovers that the President has assembled a secret army of vigilantes to suppress dissent and is setting up concentration camps in which to imprison protesters, hippies and other “social undesirables.”
Themes: USA, authoritarianism, dissent suppression, political corruption, social control

Title: Amor de Perdição / AKA Doomed Love
Description: The third installment of Oliveira’s Tetralogy is a brilliant and devastating portrait of young lovers tragically separated by a bitter feud between their aristocratic families. InDoomed LoveOliveira tested his belief in a creative merging of theatrical, literary and cinematic narrative traditions. His radical approach to adaptation captures the multilayered language of Camilo Castelo Branco’s eponymous epic novel to offer a virtual phenomenology of life and love in 18th century Portugal. After a disastrous premiere on Portuguese television, the theatrical release of Oliveira’s re-edited version was quickly hailed as a landmark in the history of the European art film.
Genres: Art Film, Arthouse, Literary Adaptation, Mini-Series
Themes: 18th century Portugal, European art film history, HD, PORTUGAL, Portuguese television, adaptation, aristocratic feud, doomed love, virtual phenomenology of life and love

Title: Opname / AKA In For Treatment
Description: The plot follows a Dutch horticulturist from the day he leaves his farm to be admitted to hospital for a cancer treatment to the inevitable sad ending. The film displays intensely how the different people concerned deal with the taboo of cancer (which was very strong in the 70s). Nobody tells the protagonist that he is in fact fatally ill, so that he has to discover that by himself and come to terms with it.
Themes: 1970s societal attitudes, NETHERLANDS, acceptance, cancer, deception, mortality, taboo

Title: Macho Callahan
Description: A man tricked into enlisting in the Confederate army is later thrown into a hellish stockade on desertion charges. He eventually breaks out of the prison camp, reunites with his old partner and sets out to kill the man who was responsible for his being in the camp in the first place. However, after accidentally killing a Confederate officer, he finds himself pursued by a gang of vicious bounty hunters intent on collecting the reward put up by the dead officer’s widow.
Genres: Action, Prison Break, Revenge Drama, Western
Themes: MEXICO, USA, betrayal, justice, redemption, revenge, survival

Title: L’hypothèse du tableau volé / AKA The Hypothesis of the Stolen Painting
Description: Two narrators, one seen and one unseen, discuss possible connections between a series of paintings. The on-screen narrator walks through three-dimensional reproductions of each painting, featuring real people, sometimes moving, in an effort to explain the series’ significance.
Genres: Art Film, Documentary, Experimental, Narrative Experimental
Themes: FRANCE, HD, art history, human movement in art, narrative structure, paintings and reproductions, visual storytelling

Title: Sin / AKA Restless / The Beloved
Description: When Orestes returns to his Greek island homeland after several years in London to settle his late father’s estate, he begins an affair with Elena, who is married to his childhood friend Yanni. Their adultery soon leads to violence and crime.
Themes: Greek culture, UK, adultery, betrayal, childhood friendship, crime, violence

Title: The Scarecrow
Description: Just before the Salem Witch Trials, an embittered old woman, who has learned witchcraft, teams up with the Devil, and brings a scarecrow to life as part of her diabolical revenge on the judge who was once her lover.
Themes: USA, corruption, love and betrayal, revenue, witchcraft

Title: The Kingfisher Caper / AKA Diamond Hunters
Description: Set in South Africa, and based on the novel by Wilbur Smith. A family owned diamond empire is torn by the battles between an ambitious businessman and his playboy step-brother and a recently divorced sister. Power, money, love and passion all play heavily in the intrigue surrounding this battle for control.
Themes: SOUTH AFRICA, brotherly rivalry, divorce, family dynamics, love and passion, power struggles, wealth and ambition

Title: Der Fußgänger / AKA The Pedestrian
Description: Aging German man Heinz Alfred Geise is a successful business owner whose past is quite haunting; during World War II he helped orchestrate a Nazi-led bloodbath in a small Greek village. When this secret is exposed in a newspaper article, it causes his son, Andreas, to try to kill both his father and himself.
Themes: GG, SWITZERLAND, WEST GERMANY, WWII atrocities, father-son conflict, hidden past, moral responsibility, redemption

Title: Le jeu avec le feu / AKA Playing with Fire
Description: When Carolina, the daughter of wealthy banker Georges de Saxe, is reported kidnapped, it is upsetting to him even though he knows it isn’t true. The kidnappers have taken the wrong person. The banker hires Frantz a disheveled, seedy detective to find his daughter and hide her safely away. She soon finds herself in a fantasy-land whorehouse, where all kinds of extreme perversions are routinely practiced.
Themes: FRANCE, class disparity, family secrets, identity confusion, perversion and society

Title: Topper
Description: This updating of the screwball classic about two brand new angels, a carefree rich couple on earth who have a devil of a time getting into heaven stars Kate Jackson and her then-husband Andrew Stevens (who also were the film’s executive producers) in the roles played in the 1937 original by Constance Bennett and Cary Grant. It also served as a pilot to a prospective new “Topper” series (the earlier one with Anne Jeffreys and husband Robert Sterling as the invisible twosome ran from 1953 to 1956).
Genres: Comedy, Fantasy, Romantic Comedy, Screwball Comedy, TV Movie
Themes: 1930s nostalgia, USA, class differences, divine intervention, heaven vs. earth, invisibility, marriage, redemption, remake

Title: Le navire night
Description: Each night in Paris, hundreds of men and women anonymously use telephone lines that date from the German Occupation and are no longer listed to talk to each other, to love each other. These people, shipwrecked lovers, are dying to love, to escape the abyss of solitude.
Themes: FRANCE, HD, Paris, anonymity, love, occupation, solitude, telephone communication

Title: One Brief Summer
Description: A well-to-do widower with a cozy mistress and a country estate falls for his young houseguest, the inexperienced daughter of a friend of his, and marries her. This causes his daughter, who is the same age, no little distress. While the relationship of the father and daughter grows rancorous, the young bride grows attached to a handsome next-door neighbor, a farmer who is her own age.
Themes: UK, class differences, family dynamics, love triangle, marriage, social hierarchy

Title: Blood Voyage (1976) [Uncut]
Description: What is meant to be a pleasure cruise becomes a nightmare as murder follows murder on a small ship. With tension mounting, passengers and the crew turn on each other in a desperate attempt to identify the killer.
Themes: USA, betrayal, murder mystery, psychological tension, survival

Title: Les lieux d’une fugue
Description: Directed by French author Georges Perec, Les Lieux d’une fugue is an autobiographical account of the writer’s experience of running away from his aunt’s house at age 11. As much a meditation on the streets of Paris as it is personal narrative, Les Lieux d’une fugue examines how Perec’s verbal games play out alongside a movie camera.
Themes: FRANCE, Parisian streets, autobiographical narrative, childhood, memory, verbal games

Title: Stavisky…
Description: Irrestisible charm and talent help Serge Alexandre alias Stavisky, small-time swindler, to make friends with even the most influential members of the French industrial and political elite during the early ’30s. But nothing lasts forever and when his great scam involving hundreds millions of francs gets exposed, the result is an unprecedented scandal that almost caused a civil war.
Moods: charm, talent, influence, scandal, civil unrest
Themes: 1930s France, FRANCE, French society, HD, corruption, elite, financial scandal, fraud, power, social hierarchy

Title: El techo de cristal / AKA The Glass Ceiling
Description: Marta spends a few days alone while her husband is on a business trip. But she starts to get scared when she hears some mysterious steps every night on the top floor. Her neighbor will try to convince her that it is her husband’s footsteps when he returns from work, but Marta does not believe it and begins to investigate.
Themes: SPAIN, domestic isolation, investigation, paranoia, reality vs. perception

Title: Half a House / EPIC UPLOAD #2 | Uber-rare Academy Award nominated film
Description: A middleaged couple, living in divorce, is forced to spend three month in their house, which belongs jointly to them. They both try to use exactly one half of the house, but this only leads to “funny” and “dramatic” situations.
Themes: USA, cohabitation, divorce, domestic conflict

Title: Casual Relations
Description: What do vampires, Hollywood melodramas, porn films, the countdown of golden oldies, and drives down the highway with Mick Jagger on the radio have in common? Casual Relations knows. In Rappaport’s dazzling and bizarre feature-length debut, he focuses on states of imaginative possession and dispossession, demonstrating how impossible it is to separate fantasies, dreams, and realities.
Genres: Experimental, Meta-Cinema, Narrative Film, Psychological Drama, Surrealism
Themes: USA, cultural critique (Hollywood, porn films), dreams and imaginative possession, fantasies vs. reality, melodrama, narrative fragmentation, subversion of genres

Title: Salomé
Description: One of the most important films of the French experimental cinema, filmed by the Mexican director Teo Hernandez. A personal interpretation of Oscar Wilde’SSalomefrom three basic elements: the light, the color and the projection speed.
Themes: FRANCE, Oscar Wilde's Salome, cinematic experimentation, light and color, personal interpretation

Title: Kamouraska (1973) [Director’s Cut]
Description: Quebec, the 1830s and 1840s. As she attends the bedside of Jérôme, her second husband, Élisabeth recalls her youth, her marriage to her first husband, Antoine, life in remote Kamouraska where he is seigneur, their love dissolving in his mental illness and cruelties, her falling in love with an American physician, Georges Nelson, and the aftermath of Antoine’s violent death.
Genres: Arthouse, Drama, Historical Drama, Psychological Drama, Romance
Themes: CANADA, FRANCE, HD, colonialism, gender roles, historical drama, love and loss, marriage, memory, mental illness, violence

Title: The Great Waltz
Description: The film follows the life of the celebrated Austrian composerJohann Strauss Jr.— the “Waltz King” — from his early struggles to his ultimate triumph. It begins in 1840s Vienna, where Strauss’s father,Johann Strauss Sr., is a dominant orchestra-leader and expects his son to follow a conventional path rather than compose or conduct.
Genres: Biography, Historical Drama, Musical, Period Piece
Moods: triumphant, struggle, classical elegance, Viennese charm
Themes: USA, Viennese society, artistic legacy, family expectations, father-son dynamics, musical genius

Title: Hedda
Description: Henrik Ibsen’s enduring drama about a Nordic femme fatale – a neurotic, controlling, strong-willed woman who is nonetheless alluring to the males in her town. She is a solitary woman in a society held together by kinship and class. If she had had more brains she would have thought her way out of it; if she had had more courage she would have bolted long ago with Lovborg, the only true creative force in the vicinity whose manuscript she burns in the stove as if she were aborting their unconceived child.
Themes: Nordic setting, UK, class society, creativity vs. control, destruction of potential, femme fatale, gender roles, isolation in society

Artist: strives to escape alienation, while, at the same time, expressing his intense fe
Title: Peftoun oi sfaires san to halazi ki o travmatismenos kallitehnis anastenazei / AKA A Hail of Bullets
Description: A sarcastic, improvisational film, with anarchic origins, strongly cinephile flavor, and largely autobiographical in nature and content. A film director strives to escape alienation, while, at the same time, expressing his intense feelings for his wife, cinema and Greece during the restoration of democracy.
Genres: Autobiographical Film, Comedy, Cult, Improvisational Film
Themes: GREECE, Greece, alienation, cinema, democracy restoration, love

Title: Povestea dragostei / AKA The Story of Love
Description: In a medieval village lives a marriage of old people, who have always wanted to have a child. The years passed and God did not grant them that happiness. A day in the forest a UFO appears, from which mysterious creatures come out and leave a child abandoned.
Themes: ROMANIA, childhood, desire and longing, family, isolation, supernatural intervention

Title: The Bridge in the Jungle
Description: Blithely ignorant of its unforgiving landscape or its people, a hunter named Gale enters the Mexican jungle and is immediately out of his element. Resting after his first foray, Gale meets Sleigh, an aging American hunter and expatriate.
Themes: HD, USA, cultural clash, expats, loneliness, nature vs. man, survival

Title: Deadhead Miles
Description: Alan Arkin, dressed like a sea captain, aimlessly sails the American highways in his 18 wheeler mumbling manic, southern accented non sequiturs; carnivalizing roadside stops and happenstance towns while out-wierding cops and weigh stations with his new cryptic, overcoated hitchhiker buddy.
Themes: Southern humor, USA, authority evasion, carnivalization of the mundane, hitchhiking, non sequitur dialogue, road trip

Title: Towers of Silence
Description: Tells a story of a Pakistani boy’s experience and obsession with death and the Zoroastrian rituals of purification and regeneration. Shows how he develops into a young revolutionary and confronts love, religious conflict, and his own death.
Themes: PAKISTAN, Zoroastrianism, coming-of-age, death and mortality, identity crisis, love and revolution, purification and regeneration, religious conflict

Title: A Great Ride
Description: Two professional motocross riders head out on a pleasure ride from Mexico to Canada. Along the way they run into various characters like a junkyard woman, two horny hippy chicks and a teen rider and his girlfriend. When one of the bikers makes eyes at the teen’s girl he’s challenged to a race.
Themes: USA, challenge, friendship, motocross culture, road trip

Title: The Last Rebel
Description: It’s 1865 in Missouri, and the Civil War has just ended. Rebel soldiers Hollis and Matt flee from Union soldiers that want to imprison them. Hollis then frees a black man named Duncan from a lynching, and they become a trio. Later, when Hollis wins a lot of money at the pool table and Matt doesn’t get any, the two soldiers become enemies. This leads to a showdown between Hollis and Duncan against Matt and the men he has brought in to get that money.
Themes: HD, USA, betrayal, loyalty, racial injustice, redemption, survival

Title: Pafnucio Santo
Description: The controversial, sexy, surreal story of a messenger and his journey and encounters with many historical events and people like Auschwitz, the Ku Klux Klan, Frida Kahlo, Cortés and Emiliano Zapata (as a beautiful woman!).
Themes: MEXICO, cultural encounters, gender identity, historical events, social commentary

Artist: Claude Faraldo’s explorations of assaults on conformity, which he explored more
Title: Bof… Anatomie d’un livreur / AKA Who Cares: Anatomy of a Delivery Boy
Description: Experiencing something of a mid-life crisis after his beloved son marries and moves out, a factory worker makes some drastic changes in his life, moves in with the boy and his wife, and sets them all on the path to a communal lifestyle. This film is one of director Claude Faraldo’s explorations of assaults on conformity, which he explored more adventurously in his later filmThemroc.
Themes: FRANCE, HD, assault on conformity, communal living, family dynamics, mid-life crisis, non-conformity

Title: The Cat Creature
Description: When a rich man dies, some items from a collection of his are stolen- an ancient Egyptian gold amulet and the mummy that was wearing it. The police consult scholars from the local University to help with the investigation, which is taking a more serious turn as people connected with the case are killed by wounds that seem to be from a housecat.
Themes: USA, ancient Egyptian mythology, crime investigation, death and mystery, supernatural elements

Title: La dernière femme / AKA The Last Woman
Description: Gérard is an engineer who is married to Gabrielle and has a nine-month-old son, for whom he cares deeply. When his wife leaves him for feminist reasons, he is left with custody of their son. To satisfy his romantic longings, Gerard embarks on an affair with Valérie, his son’s daycare worker.
Themes: FHD, FRANCE, HD, ITALY, emotional turmoil, family dynamics, feminism, infidelity, parenthood

Title: Have a Nice Weekend
Description: A group of family and friends assembling at a small New England island home for a weekend gathering at the behest of a war veteran. After their arrival, several members of the group are systematically picked off by an unknown psychopath.
Themes: USA, family dynamics, isolation, psychological horror, war trauma

Title: Squeeze a Flower
Description: When greed threatens the livelihood of an Italian monastery, a monk flees with the formula for their special liquor.
Themes: AUSTRALIA, USA, culinary heritage, cultural identity, escape and survival, greed vs. tradition, monastic life

Title: Yumi Yet
Description: A portrait of Papua New Guinea, a society clutched by Australian colonialism and hovering uneasily between its head-hunting past and Western civilization, at the time of the nation’s independence festivities.
Themes: AUSTRALIA, Western civilization vs. tradition, colonialism, cultural identity, head-hunting past, independence, societal change

Title: The Spectre of Edgar Allan Poe
Description: Poe’s fiance, Lenore, falls into a coma and is taken for dead. She is rescued at the last possible moment from being buried alive, but the experience has driven her insane. On the advice of his friend, Dr. Forrest, Poe commits Lenore to the asylum run by Dr. Grimaldi. On a visit to the asylum, Poe and Forrest sense that something strange is going on, and decide to sneak back in after dark and investigate.
Themes: USA, asylum life, buried alive, madness, supernatural

Title: The Incredible Rocky Mountain Race
Description: An old man observes a boy bullying his playmates and treats him to a morality lesson. The man tells the story of the epic cross-country race between a young Mark Twain and his rival, Mike Fink. The bulk of the film depicts the race, which proves to be more a test of character than of stamina.
Themes: USA, character development, coming-of-age, competition vs. integrity, morality

Title: Un amleto di meno / AKA One Hamlet Less
Description: A comic spoof drawing irreverently on both the Shakespeare play and the 1877 story by Jules Laforgue. Hamlet is a would-be playwright. He suffers from inept Freudian analysis by Polonius, and Ophelia and Gertrude are women conjured up in his erotic imagination. After Claudius kills his father, Hamlet only thinks of preparing to put the theatre production in Paris that he was getting ready at Elsinore.
Themes: Freudian analysis, ITALY, Shakespearean parody, imagination vs reality, theatre production

Title: La bonne année / AKA Happy New Year
Description: Released from prison apparently under a New Year amnesty, a criminal tries to pick up the threads of a life changed not only by his daring plan to rob a jewellers in out-of-season Cannes but by someone special he met there.
Themes: Cannes, FRANCE, France, HD, amnesty, jewelry heist, love and crime, post-prison life, redemption, relationships, second chances

Title: Murder at the World Series
Description: A troubled young man, bent on avenging the Houston Astro baseball team’s rejection of him, plots a bizarre kidnapping during the final two games of the World Series in Houston and places the lives of five innocent women in jeopardy.
Themes: USA, baseball, innocence at risk, kidnapping, revenge

Title: The Little Ark / EPIC UPLOAD!
Description: This heart-rending family-oriented drama chronicles the adventure of two Dutch children who temporarily lose their father and mother during the great flood of 1953. Fortunately, they and their animals are taken in by a salty old boatman who helps them reunite with their father.
Themes: USA, childhood resilience, disaster recovery, family bonds, reunion, survival

Artist: described as a “somewhat immoral street urchin who is very much like myself”
Title: Badou Boy
Description: A sarcastic look at Senegal’s capital that followed the adventures of what the director described as a “somewhat immoral street urchin who is very much like myself”. The contest pits the non-conformist individual against an absurdly caricatured policeman who pursues the protagonist through comedically improbable scenarios.Badou Boycelebrates an urban subculture while parodying the state.
Themes: SENEGAL, individual vs. authority, non-conformity, self-reflection, urban subculture

Title: Terror in the Sky
Description: On a transcontinental flight, the flight crew suffer from food poisioning and become incapacitated. To save the aircraft, the cabin crew locate a passenger with flying experience. He is coached by an experienced pilot on the ground.
Themes: USA, aviation, emergency response, teamwork under pressure

Title: The Invincible Six
Description: Based on the story “The Heroes of Yucca,” The Invincible Six features a ragtag group of jewel thieves who become the unofficial guardians of a small Iranian village. When bandits attack the village, looking for the body of their former leader and his treasure map, the Invincible Six are there to save the day, wooing the local females and frustrating the new leader of the bandits.
Themes: IRAN, USA, banditry, conflict resolution, loyalty, protection, treasure hunting, village life

Title: Beaubourg, centre d’art et de culture Georges Pompidou
Description: On 31 January 1977, the Centre George Pompidou opened its doors to the public in Paris. Three months later, on 6 May, Roberto Rossellini wrapped up the editing of a 54-minute film that testified to the public’s response to the project. The great Neo-Realist filmmaker was proposed by Jacques Grandclaude, spreadhead of the Communauté de Cinéma, to the French Ministry of Foreign Affairs to celebrate the opening of the building designed by Renzo Piano and Richard Rogers.
Themes: FRANCE, Neo-Realism, architecture, cultural heritage, institutional celebration, public response

Title: The Owl and the Pussycat
Description: Trying to keep her head above water, New Yorker Doris accepts money for sexual favors from men who occasionally visit her apartment, a practice that bugs her neighbor, Felix, an unsuccessful writer. Felix gets her booted, but when she complains to him, he lets her bunk at his place. Reserved Felix and outlandish Doris have little in common, but as they’re forced to spend more time together, their proximity leads to an increasing mutual fondness.
Themes: USA, class differences, mutual growth, roommates, sexual politics, unconventional relationships, urban living

Title: My Father’s House
Description: A magazine editor suffers a heart attack and begins to reminisce about the happy childhood he spent with his father. The memories cause him to wonder about the family he has raised since growing up.
Themes: USA, childhood memories, family dynamics, father-son relationships

Title: Snatched
Description: The wives of three wealthy men are kidnapped and held for a $3 million ransom-$1 million for each of them. One of the wives, a diabetic, needs her insulin medication or she will soon die. One of the husbands refuses to pay his share of the ransom because he believes that his wife is having an affair with another man.
Themes: USA, blackmail, class disparity, family dynamics, marital infidelity, medical emergency

Artist: and actors with a proposition that their story is more interesting than the play
Title: Six Characters in Search of an Author
Description: A television adaptation of the play by Luigi Pirandello, which transposes the traditional theatrical setting to a television studio. As a group of actors prepare for a rehearsal of a TV adaptation of the Pirandello play – `The Rules of the game’ – six strangers mysteriously appear and confront the director and actors with a proposition that their story is more interesting than the play at hand.
Genres: Drama, Metafiction, Psychological Thriller, TV Movie, Theatre
Themes: USA, identity, metatheatre, performance and illusion, reality vs. fiction, strangers and intrigue

Title: Goodbye, Franklin High
Description: During his senior year, a high-school student must deal with his girlfriend and parents, and make a difficult decision whether to do the safe thing and go to college or try for a glamorous baseball career.
Themes: USA, choice, family dynamics, glamour vs. stability, youth

Title: Wieczne pretensje / AKA Permanent Objections
Description: Franek, a petty criminal and gambler, befriends a man who performs inspections of meat plants. His assistant. Rysio, suspects wrongdoings at the slaughterhouse. Franek, despite his age, cannot find a place in life, with nothing achieved and, what’s more, still not knowing what to do.
Genres: Arthouse, Character Study, Drama, Social Drama
Themes: POLAND, alienation, corruption, failure, friendship, industrial life, midlife crisis, petty crime, purpose in life

Title: Sérail / AKA Surreal Estate
Description: Eric is a novelist whose imagination is unusually powerful. While exploring a run-down mansion in the French countryside, looking for sites for his stories, he meets a girl dressed in clothes from another time. Returning to the site, he encounters a modern woman who says he probably met a ghost. He is fascinated by the situation which cannot be what it seems.
Themes: FRANCE, exploration of the past, ghostly encounters, imagination vs reality, time travel

Title: Dalmas
Description: Bert Deling’s surreal, button-pushing and hallucinogenic paean to the emerging possibilities of avant-garde and homemade filmmaking. Telling the tale of a violent ex-cop searching for the man who killed his partner, the film takes an unexpected turn when he encounters drug lord Plastic Man and a tribe of LSD enthusiasts. What follows is both literal and metaphorical mayhem as the boundaries of the film start collapsing and our idea of what’s real is pushed to its very limits.
Themes: AUSTRALIA, avant-garde filmmaking, crime and punishment, drug culture (LSD), homemade filmmaking, metaphorical mayhem, reality distortion, tribal communities, violence and redemption

Title: Xica da Silva
Description: The uncommonly sexy, clever and ambitious slave Xica Da Silva won her independence and much more in mid-19th-century Brazil by using her feminine wiles and her lovemaking prowess to induce the Portuguese town governor to grant her freedom. In so doing, she became a legend and an inspiration to Brazil’s large population of slaves and (eventually) ex-slaves. This film tells her story.
Themes: BRAZIL, Brazil, HD, feminine power, freedom, historical, slavery, social change

Title: …ere erera baleibu izik subua aruaren…
Description: A set of words without any meaning, forms the title of the first and only feature film in the history of Spanish cinema made entirely by hand-painting directly on celluloid.
Themes: SPAIN, cinematic innovation, hand-painted film, visual art

Title: Mandy’s Grandmother
Description: A young tomboy and her prim grandmother quickly forget the disappointment of their first meeting and become friends.
Themes: USA, family bonds, friendship, generational differences

Title: Monkeys in the Attic
Description: Four self-absorbed artists living together in one mad house as if in one mad mind but all somewhere else who knows where, lost each in themselves and in their search for the perpetually elusive love or joy or hope or whatever… in a night of craziness where nothing makes sense, their art, their fantasy, their relationships, all sheathed in a veneer of filmic formality and all amounting to nothing, dreams, vanishing like the vapours of night at sunrise.
Moods: self-absorbed, madness, craziness, elusive, dreamlike, vanishing
Themes: CANADA, artists' lives, existentialism, fantasy vs. reality, film as art, isolation, love and joy, relationships, search for meaning

Title: Kitamura Toukoku: Waga fuyu no uta
Description: This biopic is centered on New Year’s Day of 1894, when Kitamura is recovering from a suicide attempt. Japan is then under the spell of fervent patriotism because the government wants to build up public support for a war with China. Kitamura’s literary friends and militant comrades come to visit. They wonder why Kitamura wants to kill himself. Kitamura at first refuses to receive them, then he sits down with them and looks back on his days as a civil rights militant, his stormy love life and his ardent but destructive desire to live literature to the full.
Themes: JAPAN, civil rights militancy, literature as a way of life, love and desire, personal vs. national identity, suicide and existential reflection, war and patriotism

Title: How Come Nobody’s On Our Side?
Description: A pair of movie stuntmen and some bit players take to the road after becoming disgusted with the self-indulgent Hollywood lifestyle.
Themes: Hollywood critique, USA, anti-establishment, road trip

Title: That Certain Summer
Description: The first US teleplay to deal sympathetically with homosexuality. Divorced San Francisco contractor Doug Salter is looking forward to a summer visit from his fourteen-year-old son Nick, who lives in Los Angeles with his mother Janet. The boy does not know that his father is gay and committed to Gary McClain, his life partner of several years.
Themes: GG, USA, coming out, family dynamics, homosexuality, identity, parent-child relationships

Title: Toute une vie / AKA And Now My Love
Description: The movie follows the lives of a woman and a man starting from several generations earlier. The story spans a whole century and several continents.
Themes: FRANCE, HD, cross-continental journey, family legacy, generational change, historical progression

Title: Film About a Woman Who….
Description: Yvonne Rainer’s landmark film is a meditation on ambivalence that plays with cliché and the conventions of soap opera while telling the story of a woman whose sexual dissatisfaction masks an enormous anger.
Themes: FHD, HD, SUBTITLED, USA, anger, cliché deconstruction, feminist critique, sexual dissatisfaction, soap opera conventions

Title: Racquet
Description: Centred around a former tennis champ who swings with the girls and volleying straight sets with the rich and famous while set on owning his own tennis court.
Themes: HD, USA, ambition, celebrity culture, class disparity, self-reinvention, sports and wealth

Title: Mrs. Sundance
Description: The girlfriend of the Sundance Kid is on the run with a price on her head, when she hears rumors that the Sundance Kid may still be alive.
Themes: USA, outlaws, redemption, romance, survival

Title: And the Word Was Made Flesh
Description: When a scientist finds a cocoon a woman emerges from it. She is being chased by two faceless monsters who are collecting specimens for a museum.
Themes: AUSTRALIA, body horror, faceless entities, specimen collection

Title: Roseland
Description: “Roseland” is made up of three stories, sometimes connecting, all set in the famed New York dance palace, and all having the same theme: finding the right dance partner.
Themes: USA, dance culture, love and relationships, self-discovery, urban life

Title: Unman, Wittering and Zigo
Description: A new teacher arrives at a school and begins to suspect his predecessor was murdered by the pupils, though his suspicions are written off as paranoia. He sets out to prevent the same fate from befalling him.
Themes: UK, murder, paranoia, predecessor's fate, school setting

Title: Le camion / AKA The Lorry / The Truck
Description: Initially conceived as the story of an older woman hitching a ride with a trucker, bemoaning the demise of the revolution and the impoverished state of society (“the world has gone to rack and ruin”),Le Camionis the film that ensued when Duras couldn’t find a suitable actress for the lead. Instead, she and Gérard Depardieu sit at a table and read from the script, discussing the film that might have been, with periodic cutaways to a truck driving along the highway at night.
Themes: FRANCE, aging and memory, dialogue-driven storytelling, existential contemplation, improvisation in filmmaking, meta-narrative, revolutionary disillusionment, social decay

Title: Prebroyavane na divite zaytzi / AKA The Hare Census
Description: The daily routine in the village of Yugla is shaken by the statistician clerk Asenov who come with a mission to take the census of the hares in the locality. He makes the village mayor Bay Georgi mobilize the local men in realization of the absurd task. On the very day all the village men are in the field. The mayor, the teacher, the veterinarian… even an old men join the group. Naturally all the efforts failed in fulfilling the mission since not a single hare came into sight.
Themes: BULGARIA, authority, census, community effort, failure, village life

Title: SST: Disaster in the Sky / AKA SST: Death Flight
Description: The world’s first commercial supersonic flight, Maiden I, is about to take off from New York to Paris, filled with a group of contest winners and crew. Unfortunately, Les Phillips, an engineer, fed up with being dissed by the plane’s designer, Willy Bassett, sabotages the plane’s hydrualic system, causing it to malfunction during flight. And as if that isn’t bad enough, Ralph Therman, an epidemioligist from WHO, has the plane transport a highly infectuous Senegal flu, turning the plane into a flying quarantine zone.
Genres: TV Movie, disaster film, drama, sci-fi, thriller
Themes: USA, aviation disaster, engineering sabotage, epidemiology, human error, international travel, professional rivalry, quarantine

Title: Gunki hatameku motoni / AKA Under the Flag of the Rising Sun
Description: A war widow searches for the true reason behind her husband’s death, and why she’s been unable to collect survivor benefits. It turns out he has been classified a traitor and a deserter, and the exact cause of his death listed as ‘unknown’. She sets out on a quest to find the people he served with, to determine the real story behind the government smokescreen.
Themes: JAPAN, betrayal, government corruption, survivor's journey, truth-seeking, war trauma

Title: Strategia del ragno / AKA The Spider's Stratagem
Description: Athos Magnani, a young researcher, returns to Tara, where his father was killed before his birth, at the request of Draifa. The father, also named Athos Magnani and looking exactly like the son, was killed by a fascist in 1936 — or so says Draifa (his mistress), the town statue, and everyone in town. As the son untangles the web of lies this story is constructed from, he finds himself ensnared in the same web.
Genres: Drama, Historical Drama, Mystery, Politics, Psychological Thriller
Themes: HD, ITALY, betrayal, fascism, father-son relationships, historical revisionism, identity, lie, memory, small-town secrets, truth

Title: The Face of Fear
Description: A young Iowa schoolteacher, thinking she is dying of leukemia, goes to San Francisco, where she hires a mob killer to take her life. However, she soon changes her mind, and with the help of the local police, tries to find the killer before he fulfills his part of the bargain.
Themes: USA, crime and redemption, ethical dilemmas, mortality, self-determination, urban isolation

Title: The Trial of the Moke
Description: In 1881, Lt. Henry Ossian Flipper, the first African-American man to graduate from West Point, went on trial for embezzlement, a crime he didn’t commit. Desperate to prove his innocence, Flipper finds that he has little chance of clearing his name.
Genres: TV Movie, biographical, courtroom drama, drama, historical
Themes: USA, historical drama, innocence vs. accusation, military corruption, racial injustice

Title: Lies My Father Told Me
Description: Period drama set in Montreal in 1925. Tells the story of a six-year-old boy, David, his grandfather, a horse called Ferdeleh and the comic-tragic ambitions of the boy’s father who wants to become a rich man.
Themes: CANADA, FHD, GG, HD, SUBTITLED, aspiration, childhood, family dynamics, social class

Title: Themroc
Description: Made without proper language, just gibberish and grunts, this is an absurdist comedy about a man who rejects every facet of normal bourgeois life and turns his apartment into a virtual cave.
Themes: FRANCE, anti-consumerism, isolation, rejection of bourgeois life

Title: The Werewolf of Woodstock / Aired on ABC as part of the anthology series "The Wide World of Mystery".
Description: At the site of the 1969 rock concert at Woodstock, New York, an electrical charge turns a local farmer into a murderous werewolf.
Themes: 1960s, USA, mythology in modern society, rock culture, transformation, violence, werewolf mythology

Title: Diabel / AKA The Devil
Description: Young Polish nobleman Jakub is saved from imprisonment by a stranger. In return, the stranger wants to obtain a list of Jakub’s fellow conspirators. As he follows his mysterious savior across the country, Jakub is affected by the overall chaos and moral corruption; he goes insane and becomes a mass murderer.
Themes: FHD, HD, POLAND, betrayal, conspiracy, moral corruption, psychological descent

Title: Mid-Knight Rider / AKA Hollywood Knight / Hard Knocks
Description: Guy Montgomery is a male prostitute and on the run. Desperately trying to escape his tragic and sometimes violent past. After being drugged and abused by a famous actress and her husband, he awakens to a surge of pain and anger which erupts, stopping only with the death of the husband – or so he believes.
Themes: USA, abuse, escape, past trauma, redemption, violence

Title: Emitai
Description: The film, Emitai, is the story of the silent resistance among a Diola tribe in West Africa in early World War II. Desiring a strong army, the French tear through villages rallying up men to be transported to France while leaving the women behind to tend to children and the elderly. The tension between the army and villagers grow when the French demand access to their rice crop. The village resistance ends in violence and uproar because rice is not only essential but also sacred to the village.
Themes: SENEGAL, colonialism, cultural identity, gender roles in conflict, resistance, sacredness of tradition, war and its impact on civilians

Title: Gal Young ‘Un
Description: This film, set in Florida in the 1930s, involves an independent woman who marries a charming but wastrelly man much younger than herself. She tries to maintain equilibrium in the relationship despite her husband’s obvious preoccupation with the “gal young’un” who works as their housekeeper.
Genres: Drama, Romantic Drama, Slice of Life
Themes: 1930s America, FHD, HD, SUBTITLED, USA, age gap in relationships, domestic tension, independence vs. dependency, marriage, social class

Title: Les Naufragés de l’île de la Tortue / AKA The Castaways of Turtle Island
Description: Bonaventure is a semi-competent travel agent who makes up stories about his great romantic adventures to tell to the girls in his office. One evening, he meets a woman who has the same name as one of his made-up romances, and they share a for-real one-night encounter. Afterward, he thinks up a scheme for a unique tour situation in which people might pay for a limited-time visit to a completely undeveloped island where they will be forced to become modern-day Robinson Crusoes.
Themes: FRANCE, adventure, deception, fantasy vs reality, isolation, modern-day Robinson Crusoe, romance

Title: Caro Michele
Description: Michael is the younger son of a middle-class family, a strong-willed and free-thinking fellow, who is off in some distant country fighting for a revolutionary cause. Everyone in the family writes to him, describing the events of their lives, as they drift into a kind of conventionality which would perhaps have horrified them earlier. Only Michael’s girlfriend Mara, the mother of his child, retains her independence, even though it is through the help of Michael’s increasingly conventional friends and family that she survives.
Genres: Drama, Family Drama, Political Drama, Slice of Life
Themes: ITALY, family dynamics, independence and conformity, motherhood and survival, revolution vs. conventionality, war's impact on personal life

Title: Actas de Marusia / AKA Letters from Marusia
Description: This Mexican film re-creates a dark chapter in the history of Chile. The scene is a small Chilean mining town in 1907. Suffering under the despotic rule of the British mine owners, the workers stage a revolt. The government’s solution is to utterly destroy the town rather than allow the rebellion to spread. Letters from Marusia was adapted from a novel by Patricio Manns, which in turn was based on eyewitness accounts of the 1907 massacre.
Themes: MEXICO, class struggle, colonialism, historical injustice, labor rights, massacre, oppression

Title: La tierra prometida / AKA The Promised Land
Description: During the socialist government of Marmaduke Grove in 1932, a group of villagers decide to take some land in the area of ​​Palmilla. Almost like a mythical journey, problems arise when seated and in a position to bring the socialist ideal in the population. Everything becomes more complicated with rumors that the reactionary forces have overthrown the socialist government. A movie that because of the coup was not released in Chile and was only terminated by Littin in exile in Mexico.
Themes: CHILE, CUBA, coup d'état, land reform, political struggle, socialism

Title: Gradiva
Description: After unearthing an ancient bas-relief sculpture of the gradiva or ‘woman who walks’ a young archaeologist begins to dream of the figure eluding him, while also pursuing a mysterious woman who eludes him in his waking life. Based on the novella by Wilhelm Jensen.
Themes: ITALY, archaeology, mythology, obsession, reality vs. dream, symbolism

Title: Things in Their Season
Description: A sentimental family drama about the growing apart — and together — of a household of Wisconsin dairy farmers when the son decides to get married and move away and the mother discovers she has incurable leukemia and only a short time to live.
Themes: USA, family dynamics, growing apart and together, leukemia, marriage, mortality, rural life

Title: Renaldo & Clara
Description: Filmed in the autumn of 1975 prior to and during Bob Dylan’s Rolling Thunder Revue tour – featuring appearances and performances by Ronee Blakley, T-Bone Burnett, Jack Elliott, Allen Ginsberg, Arlo Guthrie, Ronnie Hawkins, Roger McGuinn, Joni Mitchell, Mick Ronson, Arlen Roth, Sam Shepard, and Harry Dean Stanton – the film incorporates three distinct film genres: concert footage, documentary interviews, and dramatic fictional vignettes reflective of Dylan’s song lyrics and life.
Genres: Biographical, Concert Film, Documentary, Drama, Musical
Themes: 1970s Americana, USA, artistic collaboration, bohemian lifestyle, folk and rock music evolution, lyrical storytelling, music and counterculture, personal narrative

Title: Boquitas pintadas / AKA Painted Lips / Heartbreak Tango
Description: Nana, the heroine of this movie, has only one chance at real love, with the good-looking ladies’ man Juan Carlos. In this film, based on Manuel Puig’s novel Heartbreak Tango, Nana is too shy to go to bed with him and reveal that she is not a virgin. When he discovers that he has tuberculosis, he is forced to leave town for an extended rest-cure, and when he returns, she has since married a wealthy man who takes her with him to glamorous Buenos Aires. Years after Juan Carlos’ death, she journeys with her children to the town where he died. In her old age, though, she has virtually forgotten their love.
Themes: ARGENTINA, aging and memory, class differences, fate and regret, love and loss, shyness and vulnerability

Title: Northern Lights
Description: The bitter-sweet story of young lovers caught up in a political struggle waged by farmers against the grain trade, the banks, and the railroads. Set in North Dakota during 1915-16, a largely forgotten era of American history.
Genres: Arthouse, Drama, Historical Drama, Political Drama, Romantic Drama
Themes: American history (early 20th century), SUBTITLED, USA, class conflict, corporate exploitation, farmers' struggle, historical injustice, love and politics, rural life

Title: Ace Eli and Rodger of the Skies
Description: The story of Ace Eli Walford, a 1920s stunt flyer who barnstorms around the country, taking his eleven-year-old son Rodger with him as he goes from town to town. The place is rural Kansas, and the time is midsummer in the early nineteen-twenties, not long after World War I.
Themes: USA, coming-of-age, father-son relationship, freedom and adventure, post-World War I era, rural life

Title: Canli hedef / AKA Live Target
Description: An old mafia leader tries to quit his old bloody past after getting out of jail but his rivals don’t let him to realize his plan. Asim Mavzer, acted by Yilmaz Guney has to take the revenge of his daughter.AKA Live Target
Themes: TURKEY, father-son relationships, mafia life, prison and rehabilitation, redemption, revenge

Artist: of a school is surprised by a student when murdering someone
Title: The Student Connection (1974) [English dub] / AKA Un par de zapatos del '32
Description: The director of a school is surprised by a student when murdering someone. He must kill the only eyewitness too, but his only clue is a number 32 shoeprint.
Themes: ITALY, SPAIN, concealment, crime, identity

Title: Raggedy Ann & Andy: A Musical Adventure
Description: This animated children’s film tells the story of the two popular dolls who go off to find a pretty French doll who has been stolen by pirates. Many songs from popular children’s composer Joe Raposo ensue, including: “I Look and What Do I See!,” “No Girl’s Toy,” “Rag Dolly,” “Poor Babette,” “A Miracle,” “Ho-Yo,” “Candy Hearts,” “Blue,” “The Mirage,” “I Never Get Enough,” “I Love You,” “Loony Anthem,” “It’s Not Easy Being King,” “Hooray for Me,” “You’re My Friend,” and “Home.”
Genres: Adventure, Animation, Children's Film, Fantasy, Musical
Themes: USA, childhood, coming of age, dolls and toys, friendship, humor, music and song, piracy

Title: La vraie nature de Bernadette
Description: A woman imbued with naturalistic and libertarian theories leaves her city home to live in the countryside with her young son. There she meets a litigious farmer who fights against the banks and the government, in which she will be somehow involved. Meanwhile, practising her theories, she makes some old men very happy and, by chance even passes as a saint!
Themes: CANADA, father-son relationships, happiness through simplicity, libertarianism, nature vs. urbanization, rebellion against authority, spirituality

Title: Something Wicked This Way Comes
Description: A strange carnival arrives one night in October. Two boys are the only ones in town who know the secret of Mr Black and Mr. Puma.
Themes: UK, childhood, duality, secrets, supernatural

Title: Dead Men Tell no Tales
Description: A photographer is chased by professional killers who have mistaken him for the person they’re really after.
Themes: USA, identity confusion, mistaken identity, pursuit

Title: Du côté d’Orouët
Description: A patient observation on the adventures a group of three young girls spending their three-week summer vacation at a small village, a quotidian that includes cooking, excursions, playing cards and going out with guys, enjoying the simple pleasures life has to offer.
Themes: FRANCE, female friendship, simple pleasures, small village life, summer vacation, youth

Title: Kamome-yo, kirameku umi o mitaka/meguri / AKA Oh Seagull, Have You Seen the Sparkling Ocean?: An Encounter
Description: High-rise window cleaner Katsuo sees an office lady in an awkward situation. He is smitten and pursues her hotly. They are both running away from a hopeless life in their villages. They fall in love, but she gets raped. Tragedy ensues.
Themes: JAPAN, escapism, love and loss, sexual violence, social isolation, urban-rural contrast

Title: Pravda
Description: Co-directed by Godard with the Dziga Vertov group in 1969, ‘Pravda’ is a direct attack to revisionism and socialist imperialism. With his usual heterogeneous collage of images taken from real life, the film is structured in a sort of letter that a man writes to a woman called Rosa from Bulgaria and later from Czechoslovakia.
Genres: Collage Film, Documentary, Experimental Film, Political Film
Themes: Bulgaria, Czechoslovakia, FRANCE, WEST GERMANY, anti-revisionism, epistolary structure, political critique, revisionism, socialist imperialism

Title: W.W. and the Dixie Dancekings
Description: W.W. is a happy-go-lucky crook who makes his living robbing gas stations through the drive-up windows. The Dixie Dancekings are a country music band trying to get their first big break. W.W. crosses paths with the Dixie Dancekings when he hijacks their car (and them) to help him rob a bank. At first, the band resists. However, when they discover how much money they make, they begin helping out voluntarily in order to finance their big break. At the same time, W.W. takes a liking to them and uses his natural charm and smooth-talking ways to help them start down the road to stardom.
Themes: USA, ambition, crime and redemption, friendship, heist, music industry

Title: Happy Birthday, Wanda June
Description: Big game hunter Harold Ryan returns home to America, after having been presumed dead for several years. He finds that his wife Penelope has developed relationships with men very much unlike himself, including a vacuum salesman and a doctor.
Themes: USA, identity crisis, marital infidelity, social class

Title: Deváté srdce / AKA The Ninth Heart
Description: Martin, a poor student, volunteers to go on a quest to find a cure for the princess Adriana, who is stricken with a strange illness. Unknown to Martin or anyone else, the princess is actually under the spell of the powerful magician Andlobrandini, who is preparing a rejuvenating elixir made from the blood of nine children’s hearts.
Themes: CZECH REPUBLIC, magic and curses, power and corruption, redemption, sacrifice

Title: Ukroshcheniye ognya / AKA Taming of the Fire
Description: Year 1930. Young Andrei Bashkirtsev, just graduated from school, goes to Moscow with drawings of a self-constructed airplane. Illiterate drawings are rejected, but Bashkirtsev does not abandon his dream – to build a jet-engine aircraft. But life disposes differently, and Bashkirtsev becomes the first person in Soviet rocket engineering.
Themes: Soviet history, USSR, dream vs. reality, innovation and engineering, persistence vs. adversity, personal ambition

Title: Ritratto di borghesia in nero / AKA Nest of Vipers
Description: A background of rising fascism in Venice in 1930s. A music-student, Matthias, mixes with the town’s bourgeoisie and falls in love with a mature teacher, Carla, the mother of his friend Renato, and then with his young colleague, Elena. The themes include blackmail, betrayal, seduction, and eventually murder.
Themes: ITALY, betrayal, blackmail, class struggle, fascism, love triangle, murder, power dynamics, seduction

Title: Bunny O’Hare
Description: Bunny O’Hare is an elderly woman living in New Mexico who is trying to keep herself afloat financially while supporting her two grown children, both of whom have fallen into bad straits. Thanks to a mistake by her bank, Bunny is evicted from her home, which is quickly torn down, but not before one Bill Green can repossess her commode. Bunny is left with nowhere to go, and Bill allows Bunny to tag along in his trailer after he leaves. Bunny soon learns that Bill was once a bank robber who is still on the run from the law, and she persuades him to show her the ropes so she can steal her nest egg back from the bank.
Themes: USA, aging, crime and consequences, family dynamics, financial struggle, redemption, resilience

Title: Foxtrot
Description: Wealthy European aristocrat Liviu and his wife, Julia escape the harsh reality of WWII by vacationing on a tropical island. Due to unforeseen circumstances involving their ship, they end up stranded on the island along with their servants, Eusebio and Larsen. They run around lawlessly and slowly run out of food. Soon the servants revolt and the wealthy couple discover that they can’t escape the war.
Themes: MEXICO, UK, class struggle, colonialism, survival, war's impact on society

Title: La califfa
Description: The workforce of a factory owned by the patriarchal boss Doberdo are on strike. During a police raid to break the strike, one of the workers is killed.  The dead man’s wife, La Califfa, confronts Doberdo several times. On each occasion, the factory owner and the widow feel strangely drawn to one another…
Themes: FRANCE, ITALY, attraction, class struggle, grief, patriarchy, power dynamics

Title: Abu el Banat / AKA Daughters, Daughters
Description: A man with eight daughters, and no hope of an heir, takes a mistress to console himself. He finally consults a magician who gives him a list of instructions on how to make a son. Soon his wife is pregnant again.
Themes: ISRAEL, desire for legacy, family dynamics, isolation, magical intervention

Title: Cover Girls
Description: Two world class models, Linda and Monique, work for an American intelligence agency. They are given a mission to track down an embezzler who is also being chased by a criminal, Michael. Linda falls for the embezzler.
Themes: USA, crime, espionage, love triangle

Title: Running Blind
Description: Cold War thriller set in Iceland – a British agent and his Icelandic fiancee try to protect an electronic device from falling into the clutches of the KGB.
Themes: Cold War espionage, Icelandic identity, UK, betrayal, international relations, love and duty

Title: Allegro non troppo
Description: Italy’s response to the Disney classical music/animation hybrid “Fantasia,” this film features a series of cartoon shorts set to the likes of Claude Debussy, Joseph-Maurice Ravel and Antonio Vivaldi. The animated segments feature both the humorous and fantastical: an aged satyr attempts to regain its lost youth, a bee is interrupted mid-meal by two lovers, the myth of Adam and Eve is retold and more. In between cartoons, a filmmaker struggles to complete the film.
Themes: ITALY, artistic struggle, classical music, creation myth, love and desire, mythology, youth and aging

Title: Mannen på taket / AKA Man on the Roof
Description: A police officer is murdered with a bayonet while staying in hospital. The investigative team, lead by Martin Beck, soon discover that the man had frequently abused suspects as well as civilians but that any complaints had been ignored owing to the misguided loyalty of his colleagues. The volatile mind of the killer means that Beck has to resolve the case quickly before more people are hurt.
Themes: SWEDEN, justice, loyalty, police corruption, psychological instability, violence

Title: Lo scopone scientifico / AKA The Scientific Cardplayer
Description: The Countess, a wealthy American with an addiction to card games and a large real estate portfolio, has become an expert on the card games of all the countries where she owns houses. The regional favorite in Rome is a card game called “scopone” and the Countess summons a married couple to be her adversaries. The couple are the poorest of the poor, but the Countess supplies them with one million lire to play with, promising them that they can keep the money should they beat her.
Themes: ITALY, addiction, class vs. poverty, gambling, travel and culture

Title: Jäniksen vuosi / AKA The Year of the Hare
Description: Young advertising executive Vatanen suddenly quits his job and his whole life in Helsinki, and decides to spend a while in the Finnish wilderness. A wounded hare hit by a car becomes his travel companion. Together they find reclusion in the Finnish Lapland, soon to be disturbed by a noisy group of foreign tourists and their pretentious Finnish hosts. When the hare gets ill and needs to see a vet, Vatanen must return to the city and finally face the choice between his new and former life.
Themes: FINLAND, human-animal bond, identity crisis, nature vs. civilization, reclusion and solitude, self-discovery

Title: Pound
Description: This allegorical film by Robert Downey finds humans all playing the role of animals in cages as they wait to be gassed. Flashbacks are used to tell the character’s fantasies outside the cage.
Themes: USA, allegory, fantasy vs. reality, oppression

Title: Der Fall / AKA The Fall
Description: In this Swiss detective drama a private investigator stumbles onto a case involving blackmail and an oversexed, under-aged girl. He finds the girl, and, tragically, becomes involved with her.
Themes: SWITZERLAND, blackmail, moral ambiguity, youth corruption

Title: Death Weekend / AKA House by the Lake
Description: Harry is a rich dentist who often brings women up to his rural lakehouse. One weekend, he invites Diane, a former fashion model. On their way to the house, Diane runs a gang of thugs off the road. Humiliated, the thugs track down the couple for revenge.
Genres: Crime, Erotic Thriller, Psychological Drama, Thriller
Themes: CANADA, class disparity, fashion and wealth, power dynamics, revenge, rural isolation

Title: The Meateater
Description: Psycopathic killer stalks teens at a run down, abandoned movie theatre.
Themes: USA, abandonment, horror, psychopathy, teenage vulnerability

Title: De komst van Joachim Stiller / AKA The Arrival of Joachim Stiller
Description: The film tells the story of Freek Groenevelt and Simone Marijnissen whose lives are taken over by Joachim Stiller. He’s a bizarre character who influences Freek and Simone’s lives in different ways.
Themes: BELGIUM, NETHERLANDS, identity transformation, psychological influence

Title: Kokuhakuteki Joyûron / AKA Confessions Among Actresses
Description: In this intricately layered film, the nature of actresses and what they gain from acting is explored. The lives of three actresses are laid bare, and scenes from their lives are woven in and out of interviews with each of them. Each of them has experienced a traumatic event which contributes to their particular enjoyment of becoming someone else in dramatic roles.
Themes: JAPAN, acting as therapy, emotional vulnerability, female experience, identity, performance vs. reality, trauma

Title: Deadman’s Curve
Description: On Jan and Dean’s rise to the top of the music industry, a horrible car accident leaves Jan Berry incapacitated and their dreams shattered. With the help of Dean and others, Jan slowly recovers, learning again to walk and talk. A comeback to the music industry seems impossible, but Jan’s willingness to try and with Dean the duo aim for another chance.
Themes: USA, comeback, disability and recovery, friendship, music industry, resilience

Title: Bilans kwartalny / AKA A Woman's Decision
Description: The main character is a bookkeeper, 40, who lives a quiet, uninteresting life with her husband and son of school age. She realizes that soon she won’t be needed much at home as the boy grows up and the relationship with her husband crumbles. It’s only when an embezzlement is discovered at the office and she stands up to her management, that she realizes life has more to offer.
Genres: Drama, Psychological Drama, Slice of Life
Themes: FHD, HD, POLAND, career vs. personal life, domestic life, family dynamics, midlife crisis, self-discovery

Title: Elisa, vida mi­a / AKA Elisa, My Love
Description: On a secluded farmhouse in Castile and León, Luis is reuinted with his estranged daughter, Elisa after a 20-year separation. On the farmhouse, Luis writes what appears at times to be both an autobiography and a novel. The book is played out, with memories of the past, such as when Luis walked out on his family and is mixed with fantasies about Elisa’s adult life as well as her failed marriage.
Genres: Drama, Family Drama, Literary Fiction, Psychological Drama
Themes: SPAIN, abandonment, adulthood and identity, estrangement, family dynamics, father-daughter relationships, literary reflection, memory and time, reconciliation

Title: Flesh & Blood
Description: A convict takes up boxing in prison and this brings a new meaning to his life. Once out, his trainer motivates him to become a professional boxer. He cares about only two other things, his uncomfortably close mother and absent father.
Themes: USA, family dynamics, prison life, self-improvement

Title: Barnaby and Me
Description: Norman Panama’s penultimate directorial effort, Barnaby and Me was originally filmed for Australian television. The title character is a talented Koala Bear, who is to Australian fans what Benji is to Americans. Pausing in his escape from a vengeful mobster, American con artist Caesar falls in love with Juliet Mills, whose daughter Sally Boyden keeps Barnaby as her pet. The kooky koala teans up with Caesar for a series of picaresque adventures.
Themes: AUSTRALIA, Australia, escape, family, humor, love, picaresque adventures

Title: Odette Robert / AKA Numéro Zéro
Description: In 1971, Jean Eustache films his grandmother Odette Robert. She tells him about her life: her unhappy youth, her marriage with a man who likes women, the death of her parents, of her children. She speaks about her tragedies, her life of humiliation and servitude, with a calm, almost neutral voice. In the same way she admits that “it doesn’t interest her to live”. Filmed in black and white, in a few steady shots and in a continuous way, this document is the real and moving testimony of the life of a woman of the beginning of the century.
Themes: FRANCE, autobiographical storytelling, childhood loss, early 20th-century life, elderly life, existential despair, humiliation, marriage and infidelity, parental loss, servitude, unhappy youth

Title: Dalla nube alla resistenza / AKA From the Clouds to the Resistance
Description: Two segments. The first one arranges six stories from Cesare Pavese’s “Dialoghi con Leucò”, taken from classical mythology. The second segment is taken from Pavese’s novel “La luna e i falò”: after WWII the emigrant ‘The Bastard’ comes back to his village in the Langhe (northern Italy) to find that everyone he knew has died and the war has deeply changed relationships between people.
Genres: Arthouse, Drama, Historical Drama, Literary, Mythological
Themes: FRANCE, ITALY, classical mythology, emigration and return, human relationships, loss and change, memory and identity, post-war trauma

Title: The Runaways
Description: A teenager runs away from a foster home after being accused of stealing a bicycle. During a storm a leopard escapes from a wildlife park. The boy and the leopard shelter on a farm, and the boy is able to treat the animal for shotgun wounds. When the farmer returns from holiday, the boy gets a job at a local kennels, while the leopard escapes to a nearby army firing range where it is in great danger.
Themes: USA, escape, foster care, human-animal bond, redemption, wildlife

Artist: Ivan Passer lulls us into laughter only to catch us unprepared when he wants to
Title: Law and Disorder
Description: Fed up with an escalating crime rate and an increasingly ineffective police force, blue-collar New Yorkers Willie and Cy  join a citizen’s vigilante group. Their efforts to act as an auxiliary police force are comically inept, but director Ivan Passer lulls us into laughter only to catch us unprepared when he wants to play things in dead seriousness.
Themes: USA, blue-collar life, crime, ineffectiveness of authority, vigilantism

Title: Six-Hundred & Sixty Six
Description: A group of people inside an underground complex which possesses high tech computers which tracks world events consider all options as nuclear war is at hand, air supplies may last only eight days and Biblical prophesy unfolds.
Themes: Biblical prophecy, USA, doomsday scenarios, nuclear war, survival, technology and humanity

Title: A Reason to Live
Description: Vince arranges to meet Chi Chi, who he is having an illicit affair with. But she is killed in an accident. Vince’s sister fails in a suicide attempt but dies by electrocution as fog descends on the town, after a storm in which Vince is killed.
Themes: USA, accidents, death, family dynamics, illicit affairs, suicide

Title: The Marriage of a Young Stockbroker
Description: A stock-broker is stuck in a dreary job and a marriage that’s become a dull routine. To cope with the boredom and frustration, he resorts to voyeurism and extramarital love affairs.
Genres: Comedy, Drama, Psychological Drama, Romantic Comedy
Themes: USA, coping mechanisms, extramarital affairs, marriage, routine, urban alienation

Title: Tabiate bijan / AKA Still Life
Description: For more than three decades, aging Iranian Mohamad Sardari has worked as a crossing guard at a desolate train station. Through the years, Mohamad has done little to stifle the loneliness and boredom inherent in the job. Meanwhile, back at his family home, life is similarly uneventful: Mohamad’s wife passes the time sewing night and day, especially since the couple’s son left to join the army. As time passes, Mohamad mechanically continues to do his duty.
Themes: IRAN, boredom, duty and routine, family dynamics, loneliness, time passing

Title: Der starke Ferdinand / AKA Strongman Ferdinand
Description: Ferdinand Rieche was a policeman, but found the legal constraints of that profession too stifling. He gets into the business of protecting manufacturing plants and outfits and trains his men like a private army expecting an invasion. After his excesses prove too much for his bosses, he is fired, and Ferdinand takes his strong opinions back into public life.
Themes: WEST GERMANY, authoritarianism, corruption, power dynamics, public vs. private life

Title: Mahoney’s Last Stand / AKA Mahoney's Estate
Description: In this rare and virtually unseen film we follow the exploits of Leroy Mahoney as he settles in the country leaving behind a wayward life to work his land. With plot only provided as subtext, the simple story is a dramatization of a man and his resolve to live a quite honest life farming.
Themes: CANADA, country life, hard work, redemption, self-reliance, simplicity

Title: Sbatti il mostro in prima pagina / AKA Slap the Monster on Page One
Description: A populist right-wing tabloid newspaper tries to derail the official police investigation of a brutal murder of a young girl in order to help the fascist and right-wing candidates it supports in the upcoming elections.
Themes: ITALY, election interference, fascism, institutional failure, media manipulation, moral decay, political corruption

Title: A Name for Evil
Description: Dissatisfied with the family architectural business, a man and his wife pack up and move out to his great-grandfather’s old house in the country. While trying to patch it up, the house starts to make it clear to him that it doesn’t want him there, but the local church (with some off-kilter practices of their own) seems to take a shine to him…
Genres: Dark Comedy, Gothic, Horror, Psychological Horror
Themes: USA, architectural heritage, domestic unease, family legacy, isolation vs. community, religious eccentricity

Title: Damien
Description: Terence Knapp plays Father Damien in this TV adaptation of the stage play. Damien goes to Hawaii in the late 1800s to care for lepers. Now deceased from leprosy, Father Damien appears as a spirit recounting his life as his funeral procession is carried out.
Themes: USA, historical drama, leprosy awareness, religious devotion, spiritual redemption, suffering and sacrifice

Title: The Crazy World of Julius Vrooder
Description: Julius Vrooder returns from the Vietnam War, pretending to be crazy to cope with the world which lands him in a VA hospital. He locates a tunnel where he creates a bunker existence complete with electricity while also falling in love with his doctor.
Themes: USA, isolation, love in unconventional settings, post-traumatic stress disorder

Title: Always the Innocent / AKA Lapin 360
Description: Delia Peletier has been paid to have a child for a wealthy man who wants an heir. She has the baby and turns it over to her “employer,” but subsequently decides she wants the child back. She hires three men to kidnap the child. The quartet hides out in the house of Bernard Lapin, a nuclear scientist they believe to be away on business. Lapin returns, however, and becomes romantically involved with Delia.
Themes: USA, class struggle, parental rights, science and ethics

Title: The Kansas City Massacre
Description: Gangsters free one of their colleagues being escorted to prison and kill several FBI agents and local police officers in the attempt. FBI agent Melvin Purvis puts together a special squad to track down and capture the men responsible.
Themes: USA, crime, law enforcement, redemption

Title: The Passover Plot
Description: Dramatization of the controversial best-seller that posits an alternate version of the birth of Christianity. In this version, Jesus planned for His crucifixion by taking a drug that would simulate death. After His unconscious body was placed in the tomb, a religious sect known as the Zealots would secretly steal Christ’s body from the tomb, then spread the rumor that He had risen, thus fulfilling Biblical prophecy.
Moods: controversial, dramatic, alternate history, religious tension
Themes: Biblical prophecy, ISRAEL, USA, alternate version of Jesus, birth of Christianity, crucifixion, historical fiction, religious sect, resurrection

Title: The Secret Night Caller
Description: Robert Reed portrays an IRS agent (and seemingly contented family man) who is overtaken by his compulsion to phone strangers and whisper profanities. Even worse, Reed’s access to tax-office records enables him to ring up people all over the country. The drama concentrates on Reed’s mounting realization that he has to stop himself before someone else does.
Themes: USA, authority and power, compulsion, identity crisis, mental health, moral dilemma

Title: Un borghese piccolo piccolo / AKA An Average Little Man
Description: Giovanni Vivaldi is an average middle-aged man with a secure job as a government accountant. He wishes nothing more than to set up his only son Mario, his biggest pride in the world, with the same secure government job before he eventually retires. For this, Giovanni will stop at nothing.
Themes: ITALY, bureaucracy, family dynamics, generational expectations, parental ambition

Title: Providence
Description: Clive Langham spends one tormenting night in his bed suffering from health problems and thinking up a story based on his relatives. He is a bitter man and he shows, through flashbacks, how spiteful, conniving and treacherous his family is. But is this how they really are or is it his own vindictive slant on things?
Themes: FRANCE, SWITZERLAND, betrayal, family dynamics, perception vs reality

Title: Nero Wolfe
Description: A wealthy realtor sends out dozens of copies of a book that paints the FBI in an unfavorable light. They harass her. So she turns to Nero Wolfe to get them off her back.
Themes: USA, harassment, justice vs. authority, power and corruption

Title: Mysterious Castles of Clay
Description: Punctuating the skyline of the African plains with their distinctive silhouettes, giant termite mounds support an abundance of life, and not all of it invertebrate. The Oscar-nominated Mysterious Castles of Clay provides a dramatic and enthralling insight into the diverse ecosystem these towering monoliths help sustain.
Themes: African plains, USA, diverse life forms, ecosystem

Title: Mule Feathers
Description: In this Western comedy, a sagebrush flim-flam man makes a career out of swindling naive settlers and pulling off the occasional train robbery. While our cowboy confidence man has learned his crooked game well, he also has a partner in crime — a talking horse.
Themes: USA, deception, frontier life, partnership

Title: L’Invitation / AKA The Invitation
Description: After the death of his mother, middle-aged insurance employee inherits her small cottage surrounded by a garden. Selling the cottage which is situated on unexploited ground near the center of a big city makes him a rich man and he buys a big house in the countryside. He takes some time off and decides to throw a big garden party at the house and invite all his colleagues from the office. Aided by alcohol, the guests gradually lose all their inhibitions and reveal personality traits and frustrations that they normally keep hidden.
Themes: SWITZERLAND, class differences, grief and loss, self-discovery, social dynamics, urban vs. rural life

Title: Fear on Trial
Description: The story of John Henry Faulk, a radio/TV personality of the 1950s, who was blacklisted during the McCarthy era. Faulk sued the organization that was behind the blacklisting, and the resultant trial, and Faulk’s victory, helped to put an end to the blacklisting period.
Genres: TV Movie, biographical drama, historical drama
Themes: McCarthy era, USA, blacklisting, civil liberties, free speech

Title: Secrets
Description: A sexy day in the life of a young family provides the basis of this sudsy drama. During this time the husband, the wife, and their daughter each have their own secret sexual interlude.
Themes: UK, domestic life, family dynamics, secret affairs, sexuality

Title: Nazareno Cruz y el lobo / AKA Nazareno Cruz and the Wolf
Description: Nazareno Cruz is the seventh son of a couple living in a high mountain village. According to a myth, a seventh son will become a wolf on nights of the full moon. Everyone in the village is relieved when this doesn’t happen. The boy grows up and falls in love with a beautiful girl, Griselda.
